channel.title: Electric Vehicles on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Electric Vehicles on MinWeb

item.title: SpaceX Bought 18% of Tesla Cybertrucks in Q4 2025
item.description:
If you feel like the lines between Elon Musk’s various companies are getting blurry, you aren’t alone. It seems like every time you hear about a major product success for one of his ventures, there’s a catch—or, more specifically, an internal transaction.

When data surfaced in April 2026 showing that SpaceX had purchased a massive chunk of Tesla’s Cybertruck inventory, the internet erupted. To many observers, this wasn’t just corporate synergy; it looked like a classic case of shifting money from one pocket to another to manufacture the illusion of demand. Personally, I find the whole “revolving door of capital” strategy pretty exhausting to track, but it’s clearly become the new business model.

item.title: Why Honda’s President Fears the Chinese EV Revolution
item.description:
Ever felt like you did your homework the right way, only to walk into class and realize everyone else used AI to finish it two weeks ago? That is exactly what is happening to the global automotive industry right now.

For decades, we were told electric vehicles were the future. We watched the legacy manufacturers—the giants we’ve trusted for generations—slowly dip their toes in the water. We expected them to lead the charge. Instead, we watched them fumble, pivot, and eventually hit a wall. In 2026, the frustration boiled over. Honda President Toshihiro Mibe visited a fully automated auto supplier factory in Shanghai and delivered a chillingly honest assessment: “We have no chance against this.”
